LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE ENJOINED FROM USING THE CRM DATABASE AND ORDERED TO DESTROY ALL EXISTING COPIES. PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO SUCCEED ON THE BREACH OF CONTRACT AND MISAPPROPRIATION CLAIMS. THE LEVY DECLARATION ESTABLISHES THAT THE CRM DATABASE IS A TRADE SECRET. (LEVY DEC. PAR. 15). DEFENDANT ARSENAULT ADMITS THAT SHE E-MAILED THE CRM DATABASE TO HERSELF. (ARSENAULT DEC., PAR. 8). THE DECLARATION AND SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF MR. GARDNER PERSUASIVELY SHOW THAT DEFENDANT ARSENAULT USED THE CRM DATABASE TO CONTACT MR. GARDNER AND THAT SHE WAS NOT RETURNING A MESSAGE FROM HIM. DEFENDANTS CAN STILL USE THE INFORMATION AND CUSTOMER CONTACTS THAT MS. ARSENAULT DEVELOPED PRIOR TO WORKING FOR PLAINTIFF OR THAT SHE DEVELOPED FROM SOURCES OTHER THAN THE CRM DATABASE. THE PARTIES REVISED THE ORDER TO REFELCT THE ISSUE OF WAGES AND APPROVED IT AS TO FORM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KATHY ARSENAULT AND PARCEL PENDING, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF LUXER CORPORATION FOR FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR SANCFIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,505 AGAINST PLAINTIFF LUXER AND THEIR COUNSEL, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED AND THEIR REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER TO BE ISSUED BY THE COURT COVERING CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE FULL CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO ALL DISCOVERY REQUESTS AT ISSUE IN THIS MOTION WITHIN TWENTY DAYS OF THE FILING OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION OR WITHIN FIVE DAYS OF THE FILING OF THE PROTECTIVE ORDER, WHICHEVER OCCURS LATER. Defendants Kathy Arsenault And Parcel Pending, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Luxer Corporation For Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Sancfions In The Amount Of $3,505 Against Plaintiff Luxer And Their Counsel
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NITOJ SINGH, ESQ. of Dhillon Law Group, Tel No. 415-433-1700 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS KATHY ARSENAULT AND PARCEL PENDING, INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Luxer Corporation For Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Sancfions In The Amount Of $3,505 Against Plaintiff Luxer And Their Counsel, Defendants' motion to compel is granted and their request for sanctions is denied. Subject to a protective order to be issued by the court covering confidential information, plaintiff shall provide full code-compliant responses to all discovery requests at issue in this motion within twenty days of the filing of the order on this motion or within five days of the filing of the protective order, whichever occurs later. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NITOJ SINGH, ESQ. of Dhillon Law Group, Tel No. 415-433-1700 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Plaintiff's counsel informs the Court that parties have reached an agreement on the language of a protective order, but do not have anything to present to the Court for signature at today's hearing. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF LUXER CORPORATION'S Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions, Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is granted and its request for sanctions is denied. Parties shall submit their agreed-upon proposed protective order to the Court for signature. The language of the proposed protective order should not vary from the California Rules of Court regarding motions to seal. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Set For Trial On Sep-19-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gregory Michael of Dhillon Law Group of telephone number 805-746-4833 for Plaintiff Luxer Corporation.
Fenn Horton III of Pahl & McCay telephone number 408-286-5100 for Defendants Kathy Arsenault and Parcel Pending Inc.

Mr. Horton stated that he is not contesting the tentative ruling and requested that language be added to the order with respect to his Client's ability to pursue her wage/commissions claim. Mr. Michael agreed to said request. The Court adopted its tenative ruling. The Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is granted. Defendants are enjoined from using the CRM database and ordered to destroy all existing copies. Plaintiff is likely to succeed on the breach of contract and misappropriation claims. The Levy declaration establishes that the CRM database is a trade secret. (Levy dec. par. 15). Defendant Arsenault admits that she e-mailed the CRM database to herself. (Arsenault dec., par. 8). The declaration and supplemental declaration of Mr. Gardner persuasively show that defendant Arsenault used the CRM database to contact Mr. Gardner and that she was not returning a message from him. Defendants can still use the information and customer contacts that Ms. Arsenault developed prior to working for plaintiff or that she developed from sources other than the CRM database. The parties revised the order to refelct the issue of wages and approved it as to form. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
